rxn_class_summary: Decarboxylative C(sp2)-C(sp3) cross-coupling of carboxylic acids with aryl iodides via Fe/Ni dual metallaphotoredox catalysis under 390 nm irradiation.
electron_flow: Photoexcited Fe(III) carboxylate undergoes inner-sphere LMCT to give a carboxyl radical and Fe(II); decarboxylation releases an alkyl radical that adds to (L)Ni(I)X to form (L)Ni(II)(Alkyl)X, which is reduced by Fe(II) to (L)Ni(I)(Alkyl); oxidative addition of Ar-I gives (L)Ni(III)(Aryl)(Alkyl) that reductively eliminates the cross-coupled product and regenerates (L)Ni(I)X.

probe_reagents.name: (4-tBuBpy)Ni(II)(Aryl)(NPhth) (44)
probe_reagents.observation: With FeCl3 + light gave only 2% cross-coupled product and ~6% Ar-NPhth, indicating Ni(II)-aryl is not the productive on-cycle intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: (4-tBuBpy)Ni(II)(Alkyl)(NPhth) (46)
probe_reagents.observation: Formed cross-coupled product under various conditions even without light, supporting Ni(II)-alkyl as competent intermediate; bialkyl side products implicate reductive elimination forming Ni(0).

probe_reagents.name: KNPhth (potassium phthalimide)
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 5 equiv with FeCl3/acid/base gave only 13% product, distinct from MacMillan's 67%, arguing against Ni(II)-aryl pathway.

probe_reagents.name: FeCl2 vs FeCl3 control (no light)
probe_reagents.observation: No coupled product without irradiation; only Ar-H formed, confirming light-driven Fe-mediated decarboxylation is essential.

notes: Authors explicitly consider and disfavor an alternative pathway proceeding via Ni(0)/Ni(II)(Aryl)X oxidative addition followed by radical capture; their data instead support radical capture by Ni(I)X to give Ni(II)(Alkyl)X, then reduction to Ni(I)(Alkyl) prior to Ar-I oxidative addition. Open questions remain regarding exact Fe speciation, the radical-capture step, and roles of iodide, Zn, and 4-ethylpyridine additives. Fe activation is via inner-sphere LMCT rather than outer-sphere SET typical of Ir photocatalysts.
